titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Susan Bee Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Sunday March 28, 2021 at 03:04

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc., on the Ruth Stephan Fund, 2016.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The Susan Bee papers is organized into one grouping: November 2016 acquisition, 1971-2016.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Susan Bee Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Susan Bee Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of production files for published and unpublished books, related artwork, correspondence, and computer files by or relating to Susan Bee. Production files for published works include materials relating to M/E/A/N/I/N/G; Bed Hangings (with Susan Howe); A Girl's Life (with Johanna Drucker); The Burning Babe & Other Poems (with Jerome Rothenberg); and Log Rhythms and Little Orphan Anagram (with Charles Bernstein).

bioghist.head: Susan Bee
bioghist.p: Susan Bee (born 1952) is an American book artist, painter, and editor. She is married to poet Charles Bernstein.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings, production files, correspondence, and artwork by or relating to Susan Bee.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: Published works, Unpublished works, and Computer files.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc., on the Ruth Stephan Fund, 2016.

did.unittitle: Flyer, schedule, and transcript of Susan Bee's talk
Artist statement, email correspondence and other papers
Color copies of Kate Moss collage
Email correspondence with Regis Bonvicino and Gervais Jassaud
Book proof, press release, and other papers
Announcements, email correspondence and other related papers
Correspondence and photographic prints related to cabin
Photocopies of sketches for unpublished book

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
